name: H-block twin-wing silhouette
description: Overall silhouette whose footprint forms an H: two long parallel wings joined by a shorter central connector block, leaving two open recesses (front and back courts). Classic of renaissance collegiate plans, large manor houses and institutional blocks where the two wings give long daylit ranges and the connector holds the entrance hall and stair. The two recesses become a formal forecourt and a private rear court. Consumed by the global_designer to fix the envelope before space_planner subdivides the wings and connector.
placement_rules: Footprint is two parallel wings (full depth) joined by a central connector bar.
The connector is 25-40% of the span and holds the main entrance and vertical circulation.
The two open recesses (front/back) stay empty as courts; do not fill them.
Wings and connector share full walls so the H reads as one envelope.
Wing width >= 4; 2-3 storeys suit the long ranges.

renaissance.notes: Two regular wings with a pedimented connector entrance; brick-and-stone, low hip caps.

modern.notes: Twin glazed ranges linked by a recessed glass connector; flat roofs.
